Defendants Catherine Zhang And Ecobuilding America, Llc'S Motion Of Defendants To Quash Plaintiffs Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records To Custodian Of Records, San Francisco Treasurer And Tax Collector
Off Calendar Jul-28-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Stephen Collier, (415)771-9850 for Plaintiff/Moving party Tenderloin Housing Clinic, Inc.
Camilo Artga-Purcell, (650)697-6000) Defendant/Opposing party Ecobuilding Holdings, Inc.

Not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff Tenderloin Housing Clinic, Inc'S Motion For Issue And Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Ecobuilding America, LLC is GRANTED. The subpoena is quashed in its entirety. Documents responsive to the over broad subpoena would include privileged tax records. The fact that defendants have not complied with this Court's June 8, 2016 order is not grounds to allow an over broad subpoena that requests privileged documents. Defendant's request for sanctions is denied for lack of sufficient meet and confer. See Artiga Declaration, at par. 4. Calling opposing counsel one time on a Friday and filing a Motion the following Monday is not a proper meet and confer.
Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)

Stephen Collier, (415)771-9850 for Plaintiff/Moving party Tenderloin Housing Clinic, Inc.
Camilo Artga-Purcell, (650)697-6000) Defendant/Opposing party Ecobuilding Holdings, Inc.

Argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff Tenderloin Housing Clinic, Inc'S Motion For Issue And Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Ecobuilding America, LLC is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Defendants' new objections to the RFPs that were the subject of this Court's June 8, 2016 order are improper and shall be omitted in defendants' amended responses. Any documents withheld or redacted based upon the new improper objections shall be produced or produced without redactions. All objections not initially raised have been waived. Amended, verified responses shall be served, along with responsive documents, within 5 court days of this Order. Sanctions in the amount of $3,100 are awarded against Defendant Ecobuilding America, LLC and its attorney of record, Cotchett, Pitre & McCarthy LLP payable within 5 court days of this Order.
